mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is particularly useful for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep technical knowledge of AI algorithms. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and familiarity with container orchestration.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, which is necessary for managing Flowise applications.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in your specified region with the required node count and version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure gcloud CLI is installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise using a Helm chart, ensuring that the application is installed in the correct namespace.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise --create-namespace --version 3.0.13

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in the 'flowise' namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for 'Running' status in the output.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Flowise deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Uninstall Flowise from the specified namespace.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current deployment. After upgrading, check the status of the pods.

```bash

kubectl cp flowise-backup:/data /local/backup

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise --version 3.0.13

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Backup your Flowise data.
> Upgrade Flowise to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network connection and ensure that the Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "flowise:3.0.13": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the specified image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify that the image name and tag are correct and that you have access to the image repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Flowise pod is crashing repeatedly, likely due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient cpu resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the Flowise deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new visual workflow features and improved integration with external APIs.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Node.js versions below 18.15.0.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure that your existing workflows are compatible with the new visual features.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI accessible at http://localhost:3000, with all pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n flowise'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and the necessary tools installed.
